queries: Alphabet Google GOOGL Q1 2026 earnings results

results.title: Alphabet Announces Date of First Quarter 2026 Financial Results Conference Call
results.snippet: Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ: GOOG, GOOGL) will hold its quarterly conference call to discuss first quarter 2026 financial results on Wednesday, April 29, at 1:30pm Pacific Time. The live webcast will be available and the earnings release will be posted to the Investor Relations website beforehand.

results.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) Earnings: Latest Report - Public Investing
results.snippet: Alphabet is scheduled to report its Q1 2026 earnings on April 29, 2026 with an estimated EPS of $2.62. The company previously reported Q4 2025 earnings on Feb 4, 2026 beating estimates with $2.82 EPS.

results.title: Alphabet Investor Relations
results.snippet: On April 7, 2026, Alphabet announced the date for its First Quarter 2026 Financial Results Conference Call. The earnings call is scheduled for April 29, 2026 with a webcast available to investors.

results.title: Alphabet (GOOG) Earnings Date and Reports 2026
results.snippet: Alphabet's Q1 2026 earnings is estimated for Wednesday, April 29, 2026 with a conference call scheduled at 4:30 PM ET. Analysts project EPS around $2.45 for the quarter.

results.title: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) Earnings
results.snippet: Alphabet will release its next earnings report for Q1 2026 on April 29, 2026. This follows the Q4 2025 report on February 4, 2026 which showed EPS of 2.82 beating forecasts.

results.title: Here's Why Google's $185 Billion AI Bet in 2026 Could Either Be a Masterstroke or Its Biggest Mistake
results.snippet: Google parent Alphabet projects capital expenditures between $175 billion and $185 billion for 2026, nearly double the amount spent in 2025, largely directed toward data centers, AI processors, and servers to maintain leadership in the AI race.

results.title: Alphabet (GOOGL) 2026 Research Deep Dive
results.snippet: Alphabet is investing heavily in AI infrastructure with a $20 billion bond sale and new Ironwood TPUs to reduce AI query costs, while contending with OpenAI's ChatGPT capturing nearly 18% of global search queries and potential ad revenue pressure from AI-generated answers.

results.title: Overview of Google's (Alphabet's) Strategy in 2025–2026
results.snippet: Under an AI-first approach, Alphabet plans major capex increases in 2026 for infrastructure, data centers, and custom chips like Ironwood, using profits from Search and YouTube to fuel growth with projected 14% revenue increase to around $455 billion.

results.title: Alphabet resets the bar for AI infrastructure spending
results.snippet: Alphabet forecasts 2026 capital expenditures between $175 billion and $185 billion, more than double 2025 levels, to expand AI compute capacity for Google DeepMind, meet cloud customer demand, and support other strategic investments.

results.title: Google's $180 Billion AI Bet for 2026 (GOOGL)
results.snippet: Alphabet plans to nearly double its capital expenditure spending in 2026 to a range of $175 billion to $185 billion to accelerate AI infrastructure and capabilities.

results.title: YouTube's Ad Revenue Is More Than Traditional Media Companies Combined
results.snippet: YouTube generated $40.4 billion in ad revenue last year, exceeding the combined totals from Disney, NBC, Paramount, and Warner Bros. Discovery. New research positions YouTube as the world's biggest media company by ad revenue and U.S. TV viewership.

results.title: YouTube ad revenue is down across the board in 2026
results.snippet: YouTube creators report significant declines in ad revenue across the board in 2026. Many are turning to alternative monetization methods to sustain their channels.

results.title: YouTube Revenue for Full-Year 2025 Topped $60 Billion, Making Video Platform Bigger Than Netflix
results.snippet: YouTube's total revenue for 2025 surpassed $60 billion, exceeding Netflix. Q4 ad revenue reached a record $11.38 billion but fell short of analyst predictions amid lower political ad spending.

results.title: YouTube Revenue and Usage Statistics (2026)
results.snippet: YouTube generated $36.1 billion in advertising revenue for 2024, up 14.6% year-over-year. The platform continues expanding with strong subscription growth and user engagement into 2025 and beyond.

results.title: How YouTube Ad Revenue ACTUALLY Works in 2026
results.snippet: This video explains CPM versus RPM and YouTube's 45% revenue cut for creators in 2026. It covers seasonality risks, category differences, and other factors determining actual earnings per view.
